notes: Karnok is a tempo hero. Aim to end runs before Day 13, spend gold aggressively, and be careful with Hidden Lake if your final board is still unclear. Enrage synergies stay valuable across nearly every line.

item_tier_list.description: Overall item quality ranking for Karnok. S = best, F = worst. Used as baseline signal when build synergy is ambiguous.

early.description: Establish tempo. Pick up key utility items and economy. Every board should have these regardless of final build direction.

free_rewards.lost_crate: Almost always take free medium item

early.notes: Valpak gets better the earlier you see him. Ande goes up once you find a carry. Always take Strange Mushroom, and do not get too greedy skipping bronze skills.

early_mid.description: Transition openers. Commit to a direction while staying flexible enough to pivot.

archetypes.name: Axe
archetypes.notes: Battle Axe is the carry. Support items buff or extend its damage window.

archetypes.name: Wide Weapons
archetypes.notes: Broad weapon synergy. Many items qualify - look for on-hit triggers or multi-weapon buffs.

archetypes.name: Sustain (Early)
archetypes.notes: Early sustain setup. Transitions into late Sustain build.

archetypes.name: Burn (Early)
archetypes.notes: Burn wants Burn Scar + Hunter's Sled to go late. Pivot if they do not show up by Day 7.

archetypes.name: Slow (Early)
archetypes.notes: Branches into Slow-Ammo, Slow-Non-ammo, or Slow-Poison late.

archetypes.name: Spear + Friends
archetypes.notes: Pivots cleanly into Anaconda or Red Friends late. Karst is a strong enabler if found early.

late.description: Final build archetypes. Commit by Day 7. No strong builds past Day 13 - win before then.

archetypes.name: Max HP - Weapons
archetypes.hidden_lake: Doesn't matter in most cases. Skip or take middle option.
archetypes.notes: Lost Blade needs anti-cc, Trail Markers, and usually Hidden Lake. Wild Bear is the most flexible carry. Claymore wants near-100% crit plus Diamond or extra charge.

archetypes.name: Max HP - Sigil
archetypes.condition: Offensive enchant on sigil is ideal; otherwise you need a lot of HP. Machete often needs upgrades or 2 masks.
archetypes.hidden_lake: Shortened enrage is better in midgame; later you often kill before the second enrage.
archetypes.notes: Fireclaw is a good stand-in for Machete. Trail Markers are extremely important. Recent max-health burn evidence suggests Flame Sigil shells can borrow explicit burn support from Firefly Lantern, Tinderbox, and Torch.

archetypes.name: Slow - Ammo

avoid: Radiant - may mess up slow effects

archetypes.hidden_lake: Shortened enrage - trigger Tent shielding sooner.
archetypes.notes: Bear Trap also wants extra ammo. Defensive enchant or lifesteal is usually necessary unless Ghillie Suit buys enough time. Fairy Circle is mainly for Shotgun boards. Recent Self-Slow samples reinforce Hunter's Boots, Enervating Sigil, Tent, Bear Trap, Chains, Boa Constrictor, Night Vision, and Tri-Net.

archetypes.name: Slow - Non-ammo

avoid: Radiant - may mess up slow effects

archetypes.hidden_lake: Shortened enrage is better to reactivate item effects sooner.
archetypes.notes: Defensive enchant or lifesteal is usually necessary unless Ghillie Suit or other defense buys enough time. Recent non-ammo slow evidence supports Wildroot Amulet as an additional defensive support piece. High-confidence Self-Slow builds (3 samples) confirm Boa Constrictor, Chains, Enervating Sigil, Night Vision, and Hunter's Boots as the stable core — all already present here. Bat and Trapvine also surfaced in that evidence and are credible non-ammo slow support pieces.

archetypes.name: Slow - Poison
archetypes.condition: Need good tempo. Struggles vs one-shot boards late game.
archetypes.notes: Very flexible. One active Machete or multiple slow items can work, and base poison from skills or loot matters a lot. Easy Sigil pivot. Recent Trapvine builds bridge slow-poison with sustain and max-health pieces, especially Black Mamba and Wild Boar.

archetypes.name: Sustain
archetypes.condition: Needs upgraded Stretch Pants, Flying Squirrel, and Waterskin.
archetypes.notes: Tempo build; choose between Wild Boar and Furs based on upgrades and enchants. Karst is usually better than Aurora Vista. Frog Hollow appears in recent Karnok evidence and should be considered a sustain/support bridge when the board can afford it.

archetypes.name: Sled - Weapon
archetypes.condition: Needs Spiked Collar and Hunter's Sled.
archetypes.notes: Usually transitions from a Lifting Stones early game. Off-class tag boards can be great here, but you still need enough damage output.

archetypes.name: Burn
archetypes.condition: Needs Burn Scar and Hunter's Sled.
archetypes.hidden_lake: Depends on burn per enrage cycle. Tinderbox charges 2x during enrage.
archetypes.notes: You can get creative with tag boards here, but pivot if Burn Scar or Hunter's Sled do not show up by Day 7.

archetypes.name: Giant Sling
archetypes.condition: Needs enough ammo on Giant Sling or a reload skill. Silver Sling usually needs that plus extra base damage.
archetypes.hidden_lake: Extended enrage - Sling needs extra time in damage multiplier state.
archetypes.notes: Base damage from skills or loot helps a lot.

archetypes.name: Tree Club
archetypes.condition: Needs enough crit or Tree Club will miss the enrage kill window.
archetypes.hidden_lake: Extended enrage is better so Tree Club gets more time in its damage multiplier state.
archetypes.notes: Stored charges carry into enrage. Flying Squirrel gives Tree Club targeted fly.

archetypes.name: Anaconda
archetypes.condition: Early Diamond upgrade helps a lot - 2s faster. Defensive enchant highly preferred.
archetypes.notes: Clean pivot from Spear + Friends or Karst setups. Trail Markers help the late-game board.

archetypes.name: Red Friends
archetypes.condition: Need good tempo. Can fall off late game. Strong if already running Spear + friends.
archetypes.hidden_lake: Depends on which carry you are playing.
archetypes.notes: Best if you already have tempo from Spear + Friends; later you can still transition into other weapon carries.

pivot_signals.description: Signals the player should consider pivoting build direction
signals: Missing a build's key condition item by Day 7
Board losing fights consistently despite having items
Burn build missing Burn Scar or Hunter's Sled by Day 7
Giant Sling needs ammo or a reload skill before it is stable
Anaconda scales much better with early Diamond and strong sustain
Crash Site skippable if bad board but enough gold
Temple Expedition usually more useful than Crash Site

scoring_weights.description: Phase-aware scoring weights. Early flexibility is good; late commitment matters.
